### Post by pr0phet491 on 2007-07-09
I have a problem with Compiz Fusion.  When I enable it I have no title bars, such as close, maximize, and minimize.  I've tried to add add an option to enable something in device that some have suggested(can't recall exactly what it was), I do believe I put it in "Devices"  But it ended up breaking X server, anyone have any suggetsions?  Here is my xorg file.


# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Thu Nov  9 17:56:12 PST 2006

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "PTW"
    HorizSync       30.0 - 70.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 160.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7600 GT"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "metamodes" "1152x864_75 +0+0; 800x600 +0+0; 640x480 +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

### Post by Waappu on 2007-07-09
Hi
You can try these solutions by modifying sections of /etc/X11/xorg.conf :

1. Add this entry in ' Section "Screen" ' :
```
Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
```
2. Add this section at the end of the file:
```
 Section "Extensions"
     Option "Composite" "Enable"
 EndSection
```

---

> I've tried "compiz --replace -c emerald &" as well as going to System>Preferences>Emerald Theme Manager" and it still will not show my title bars, am I doing something wrong?

Do you have Emerald themes installed ?

To install themes type in terminal```
sudo aptitude install emerald-themes
```

### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-07-12
i have a 6200 agp8x card. I use 2 seperate screens, I have compiz-fusion working on both with this xorg.conf.

[http://pastebin.com/m70d654ad](http://pastebin.com/m70d654ad)

I also, use compiz-fusion flat file instead of whatever the other option is, I don't use the gconf thingy in the commmand to start compiz as compiz.org suggests. Here's the bash script that I start compiz with.

#!/bin/sh

# Compiz startup script for version 0.4 or 0.5

compiz --replace &
gtk-window-decorator --replace &

I don't use emerald!!! Hope this help you.

### Post by psyopper on 2007-07-25
> **pr0phet491 said:**
> I've tried that as well and still no go, is there something very simple I'm doing wrong??

When you edit the /etc/X11/Xorg.conf you must do it and save it as root. Make sure the changes are there by doing this:

```
sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf
```

The two things that you should add have already been posted and they are correct. Make sure you SAVE the xorg.conf before exiting the text editor.

You MUST restart X before the changes you made to /etc/X11/xorg.conf will take affect. To restart X hit [Ctrl]+[Alt]+[Backspace]. This will forcibly close your open session and put you back to a login screen. Make sure that you save any other opened work (and bookmark this page) before restarting X.

Log in to your desktop like normal and start compiz your normal way. The command should be 
```
compiz --replace
``` and nothing else, for now. This will let you run Compiz using your current Metacity theme (this is the usual Gnome- Ubuntu theme). Once you have this working then you should install Emerald and use it's theming system.

---

### Post by psyopper on 2007-07-25
Try moving:

Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"

To either above the SubSection "Display" in Section "Screen", or in the Section "Device". I don't really think it should go in the SubSection Display for the screen, that section is used for screen sizes and refresh rates and such.

---

### Post by dannyboy79 on 2007-07-26
along with my tips from post 11, are you sure that your using the "flat file" for configuration of compiz? Also, have you ever used or installed Beryl or Emerald? If so, this is most likely your problem. THere's a wealth of info over at compiz.org about window borders not showing, that's what helped me out cause I had the same problem, but when I did what I have in post #11, all is well.

When you do the first command within a terminal

compiz --replace &

there should be a bunch of output within that termianl session, we need to see that output.

Then when you do the second command 

gtk-window-decorator --replace &

which should get your borders to show, we need to see that output as well.

---

### Post by sailor2001 on 2007-08-30
the solution is quite simple.....places/home  ctrl/h   scroll to gnome2 (maybe just gnome will do) and scroll to sessions........rename sessions to something like 'sessions.old' and then ctrl/alt/backspace
